case.title: ALASKA v. INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERAT. ENG.

opinion.h: ALASKA v. INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERAT. ENG., 393 U.S. 405 (1969)
ALASKA ET AL. v. INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS, LOCAL 302, AFL-CIO, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE DISTRICT OF ALASKA. No. 734. Decided January 20, 1969.
opinion.p: G. Kent Edwards, Attorney General of Alaska, and Edgar Paul Boyko for appellants.

J. Duane Vance for the International Union of Operating Engineers et al., and Solicitor General Griswold, Arnold Ordman, Dominick L. Manoli, and Norton J. Come for the National Labor Relations Board, appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
393 U.S. 405 VALENTI v. ROCKEFELLER, GOVERNOR OF NEW YORK, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF NEW YORK. No. 773. Decided January 20, 1969.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, Ruth Kessler Toch, Solicitor General, and Jean M. Coon, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
